kept.text:
他盯着手里的iPhone 15 Pro Max，屏幕裂了条缝，左上角。上个月摔的，一直没修。不是修不起——换个屏才两百刀——是懒得。这玩意儿现在跟块砖头似的，用着用着就发烫，卡顿，电量掉得比他前女友回消息的速度还快。

但今天不一样。今天他必须修好它，因为晚上六点，他妈要从上海飞过来。

他妈的飞机下午三点落地。

他站在Best Buy的维修柜台前，手指敲着台面，节奏越来越快。店员是个胖墩墩的黑人小哥，戴着工牌，叫德肖恩，正慢悠悠地在平板上翻着什么。

“Bro, 你这个型号的屏幕配件，仓库没货了。最快下周。”

“下周？”他声音抬高了半度，“我今天就要用。”

德肖恩抬眼看他，那种见惯不怪的、带着点怜悯的目光。“你可以去苹果店，不过那边预约排到下周二了。要不你试试亚马逊买配件自己换？Youtube上有教程。”

他妈的Youtube教程。他想起去年自己换iPad电池，把排线扯断了，屏幕直接黑掉，那台iPad现在还躺在他抽屉里，像块墓碑。

他转过身，背靠着柜台，手机攥在手里，屏幕的裂纹在灯光下像蛛网，又像血管。他想起2017年刚来美国的时候，在Best Buy买了台翻新的iPhone 7，花了三百多刀，心疼得一顿晚饭只吃了两根热狗肠。那时候他觉得，美国真他妈好，东西便宜，用得起苹果，随便打个工就能买最新款。

现在他拿着最新款，却觉得自己像个傻逼。

手机震了一下，弹出一条信息。他低头看，是妈妈发的微信语音，59秒。他没点开，不敢点。他知道她会说什么——到了到了，你忙你的，我自己找机场大巴就行，别耽误你工作——永远是这样，永远怕麻烦他，永远把自己缩成最小的那个形状。

他想起2019年回国，妈妈用的还是那台华为P9，屏幕都碎成蜘蛛网了，贴了张钢化膜继续用。他说给她换手机，她死活不肯，说还流畅着呢，说什么美国的东西贵，别乱花钱。他当时心里还挺得意——看，我用苹果，我妈用华为，这才是中产家庭该有的配置。

现在想起来，恶心。

他拨了德肖恩的电话，没人接。又拨了一遍，还是没人接。他妈的美国服务业，永远这样，修个手机要预约，看个病要预约，连他妈吃个饭都要等四十分钟。他想吃口好的，想吃点像样的、热乎的、有锅气的中国菜，但方圆十英里只有熊猫快餐，糖醋鸡块做得像裹了层塑料壳。

他打开DoorDash，输入“中餐”，跳出来的第一个选项是P.F. Chang’s，人均四十刀，还得给小费。他划掉，往下翻，看到一个叫“妈妈的味道”的店，评分4.2，图片里的红烧肉黑乎乎一坨，像焦炭。

他下单了。

不是因为想吃，是因为店名。

然后他打开苹果官网，把iPhone 15 Pro Max加入了购物车，选了午夜蓝，点了“立即购买”。付款的时候他犹豫了两秒，看了看银行余额，又看了看手机壳里那张妈妈的照片——2015年在黄浦江边拍的，她笑得像个小孩。

他点了确认。

手机修不好就换新的。别让妈看到这裂屏，别让她觉得他在美国过得不好。

别让她担心。

其实他吃什么都可以。

#美国人可以买便宜的iPhone

In case you missed this horror...
Replacing American tech workers is an entire industry with consultants and lawyers whose entire purpose is to game our immigration system. 

Read and Weep.

profile_bio.description: Advocating for the preservation of the gifts of The Enlightenment for humanity. Fighting against the neo-Marxists who want to destroy them.

quoted_tweet.text:
"Remove Obstacles and Get Result!"
That is how Erickson Immigration Group markets its PERM services, the employer-driven green card process, where a company must prove no qualified U.S. workers are available before hiring a foreign worker, making the “obstacle” the American workers.

The law requires the employer to conduct a real, good-faith test of the U.S. labor market. Attorneys and agents cannot interview, screen, reject, or control U.S. worker applicants. Why? Because the immigration lawyer’s objective is to get the foreign worker’s green card approved, not to protect U.S. workers.

Yet, EIG advertises an in-house PERM recruitment team. Then EIG-linked immigration employees appear inside major employers as “global mobility,” “immigration specialist,” or “employer contact” roles at Salesforce, DoorDash, Zendesk, eBay, Twitter and others.

So how fair is it for Americans when an immigration-firm-trained agent is embedded inside the employer to control the PERM process, while making it look like the employer is actually conducting the recruitment? They have one job and one success metric, to get the case approved, not to hire Americans. 

See the comments for more evidence!

kept.text:
“Reddit co-founder: We basically faked our first users.”

Saw this clip and went down a rabbit hole… turns out a lot of big companies did some version of this 👇 (ordered by market cap)

Uber (~$150B+) : ops made the marketplace feel alive
↳ later revealed through early ops stories, interviews, and ex-employee breakdowns

Airbnb (~$80–90B) : founders seeded & polished listings
↳ shared by founders in talks/interviews about their 2008–09 launch

DoorDash (~$70–80B) : founders fulfilled early demand
↳ talked about openly by founders when explaining early growth

Snapchat (~$20–30B) : engineered early engagement loops
↳ broken down later by product/design analyses

Reddit (~$10–15B) : seeded with fake accounts
↳ directly admitted by founders in interviews years later

Etsy (~$8–10B) : manually built early supply
↳ known through early community-building stories

Pattern:
Happened mostly 2005–2013 → only became public years later when founders started sharing “how it really worked.”

Do you think you can do the same for your SaaS?

kept.text:
From trying to make me audition to being my doordash driver (story time)

If you can understand power games, you will always be incredible successful with women.

It's borderline like being an emperor of a regime.

And the regime is just a bunch of weasel women that you rule over.

They will always try things to take the power from you, literally until your last dying breath, they can't help it.

And it is your job, through psychology and understanding game, on never giving away the power.

I picked up a retailer worker last week that gave me her number on a sticky note (she didn't have instagram).

I texted her later that evening, she responded a bit dry, so I just ghosted her.

Randomly, I ran into her at a coffee shop the next week and we talked a little bit and she made it a clear point to tell me we should hangout, while also texting me as soon as she left with great energy, so I engaged with her.

Then I brought up plans, because she verbally mentioned it at the coffee shop, and of course in classic female fashion, immediate friction trying to get me to perform and audition for her.

It's absolutely so predictable and retarded, but they are WEASELS.

And the only way to train them to behave well is to PUNISH THEM - which I did by ghosting her again.

I'm training her to behave or I'm never speaking to her again.

And of course, in female fashion when they are ignored and not pandered to, she double texts me wanting to see her.

And knowing I now I have the leverage, I put in even less effort and told her she can come thru if she wants but I'm not doing anything else.

Immediately responds with a voice note saying she would love to - LOL.

From contingent on what we're doing to coming straight to my house - this is literally women.

AND THEN, to add icing on the cake to shifting all of these stupid power games, she offers to bring me food.

I turned this girl into my doordasher from her being dry and trying to get me to chase her.

ALL BECAUSE I understand psychology and power.
